title: [CI:DOCS] man pages: sort flags, and keep them that way
body:
Enforce alphabetical ordering of command-line options in
man pages. Not as simple as with podman, because conventions
are different.

Reference: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13625

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *flouthoc*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I think I see what's happening:
![cirrus-map-main](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/159934105-956bd75b-5af9-4253-84a0-731bceed3856.png)

`Smoke` is required only by tasks that are skipped on `CI:DOCS`, so I don't think it's actually run. 

Solution(?): add a dependency to `meta` or `success`, pointing back to `smoke`, so `smoke` runs? (I really don't know what `meta` is, though, and I don't see it running either).

@cevich thoughts?

[EDIT: Never mind, I see that `success` does depend on `smoke`; the edge was removed by transitive reduction. So no, I have no idea why CI is stuck]
(Force-pushed with no changes. That seems to have unstuck CI)

> Smoke is required only by tasks that are skipped on CI:DOCS, so I don't think it's actually run.

The `depends_on` only influences the order and parallelism with other tasks, not whether or not it runs.

I'm not sure what happened, but for the last few days CI has been getting "stuck" due to github outages, so that's what I'd look for first.
